# Mexico vs South Korea Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group A

Two winners meet at Estadio Guadalajara with first place in 2026 World Cup Group A on the line. Mexico opened with a 2-0 win over South Africa, and South Korea rallied from a goal down to beat Czechia 2-1, leaving both sides on three points. Win this and you are all but through to the knockout rounds and likely topping the group.

The market makes Mexico a marginal favorite, playing at altitude in Guadalajara with the crowd fully behind them, but this is as clo

# Mexico vs. South Korea projected lineups, starting 11 for World Cup 2026 Group A game in Guadalajara

Dom Farrell

•

SN

Add The Sporting News on Google

Mexico and South Korea meet in Guadalajara, knowing that the winners can secure progress to the knockout rounds and all but seal top spot in Group A.

Co-hosts Mexico got off to a winning start at the Azteca in the tournament's opening match, beating an over-matched South Africa 2-0 in a game where three players were sent off.

South Korea came from a goal down to beat Czechia 2-1 in a vibrant display in their opening fixture.

If either of Mexico or South Korea's respective opponents from the first round of matches avoid defeat when Czechia meet South Africa in Atlanta earlier on Thursday, they will know a victory guarantees qualification as group winners.

Mexico lineup vs. South Korea: Predicted XI for vital World Cup 2026 Group A clash after red card and injury for goalscorer - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup Gameweek 2

Mexico

Guadalajara Stadium

South Korea

# Mexico lineup vs. South Korea: Predicted XI for vital World Cup 2026 Group A clash after red card and injury for goalscorer

By Anthony Nolan| 17 Jun 2026 01:57, Last updated: 17 Jun 2026 04:31

© Iconsport / PictureAlliance

World Cup 2026 co-hosts Mexico will be looking for back-to-back wins on Friday, when they face Group A rivals South Korea at Estadio Akron.

Javier Aguirre's El Tri took all three points from their opener as they downed South Africa 2-0, but they did not get away unscathed and will be missing Lokmotiv Moscow centre-back Cesar Montes, who was sent off.

Looking to fill the gap, the manager could opt to start captain Edson Alvarez at the heart of his defence alongside Johan Vasquez, with the duo shielding goalkeeper Raul Rangel.

Israel Reyes and Jesus Gallardo look set to continue at full-back this week, having been part of the XI that kept a clean sheet on matchday one.

Aguirre is likely to select Cruz Azul's Erik Lira to operate at the base of his mid

Story by

Mexico vs South Korea– Predicted lineup and team news

Mexico and South Korea continue their Group A campaign at the FIFA World Cup 2026 on Friday, with both sides aiming to secure qualification for the Round of 32 after winning their opening matches.

Mexico comes into this game in strong form following a convincing 2-0 win over South Africa in the tournament’s opening fixture. Goals from Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez thrilled supporters at the Estadio Azteca.

Advertisement

Advertisement

That result placed El Tri at the top of Group A on goal difference. Playing at home has clearly boosted confidence, and the team will look to build on that momentum with another win.

## Mexico team news

Mexico faces a defensive concern ahead of this important clash. Centre-back Cesar Montes is suspended after receiving a late red card in the first match.

Head coach Javier Aguirre is expected to adjust his defence, pos

# Mexico lineup vs. South Korea: Predicted XI for vital World Cup 2026 Group A clash after red card and injury for goalscorer

By Anthony Nolan| 17 Jun 2026 01:57, Last updated: 17 Jun 2026 04:31

© Iconsport / PictureAlliance

World Cup 2026 co-hosts Mexico will be looking for back-to-back wins on Friday, when they face Group A rivals South Korea at Estadio Akron.

Javier Aguirre's El Tri took all three points from their opener as they downed South Africa 2-0, but they did not get away unscathed and will be missing Lokmotiv Moscow centre-back Cesar Montes, who was sent off.

Looking to fill the gap, the manager could opt to start captain Edson Alvarez at the heart of his defence alongside Johan Vasquez, with the duo shielding goalkeeper Raul Rangel.

Israel Reyes and Jesus Gallardo look set to continue at full-back this week, having been part of the XI that kept a clean sheet on matchday one.

Mexico vs South Korea– Predicted lineup and team news

Mexico and South Korea continue their Group A campaign at the FIFA World Cup 2026 on Friday, with both sides aiming to secure qualification for the Round of 32 after winning their opening matches.

Mexico comes into this game in strong form following a convincing 2-0 win over South Africa in the tournament’s opening fixture. Goals from Julian Quinones and Raul Jimenez thrilled supporters at the Estadio Azteca.
